name: ETH Inventory Reset Scaleout v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit similar-asset lane for testing an inventory-reset reclaim that exits by staged scale-out rather than a single balance target. The edge should come from overextended inventory resetting into value and then continuing far enough to monetize multiple slices.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reclaim conditions remain too rare, if direction confirmation conflicts with the reclaim logic, or if partial exits cannot offset cost drag.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A flush or squeeze extension resets inventory, price reclaims value, and continuation follows for several bars before fading.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: ETH 4h often expresses cleaner reclaim structure than DOGE while still sharing enough behavioral overlap to validate the concept in the basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best similar-asset lane for this concept because reclaim continuation is easier to manage with staged exits when the market is between pure chop and mild expansion.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the reclaim only produces a one-bar bounce and not continuation, the later scale-out legs will never be reached.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries populate but late exits miss, reduce runner ambition before tightening the reclaim trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim and DI confirmation align after the reset
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using a broad reset threshold and simple directional qualifier
